items.title: IMF, World Bank, IEA urge countries to stop hoarding energy supplies, imposing export controls

items.title: China: Evergrande property developer founder pleads guilty
items.description: China's property developer giant has become its also its most indebted and defaulted on most of its liabilities and payments in 2021. Founder Hui Ka Yan "pleaded guilty and expressed remorse," the court said.

items.title: South Korea sentences 90‑year‑old woman for money laundering
items.description: The woman was helping her son launder drug revenues while he was in prison in Cambodia. The court's one-year prison term took into consideration her age and clean record prior to the laundering.

items.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first

items.title: New Paris exhibit explores borders, from walls to algorithms
items.description: A new exhibition opens this Tuesday at Paris's City of Sciences and Industry. It's called Frontier and it focuses on borders. Concrete walls, military checkpoints, surveillance algorithms: borders have a thousand faces, and none of them are truly alike. In this edition of Entre Nous we speak to the curator of the show, Delphine Heyndrickx. She tells us how her team came up with the idea and what the different sorts of borders can be.

items.title: Haiti: Several police arrested over stampede which killed 25

items.title: Intimacy coordinator Paloma Garcia Martens on 'respecting people's humanity on set'
items.description: An intimacy coordinator has spoken to FRANCE 24 about how their role is to ensure that everyone's humanity is respected on a film or television set. Paloma Garcia Martens has made a career working on some of Europe's top film and television shows, giving advice primarily to actors and actresses about how to achieve the perfect love scene with someone you probably don't want to be with, while being watched by potentially hundreds of crew. Her book is entitled "L’intimité sur un plateau", or "Intimacy on set". She spoke to us in Perspective.

items.title: Who wants what from Israel-Lebanon talks?
items.description: As Israel and Lebanon sit down for US-Mediated talks in Washington on Tuesday, expectations are running low. The Israeli side has already dismissed the idea of a potential truce, saying the priority is to get Iran-backed Hezbollah disarmed. France 24's Delano D'Souza takes a look at what each side is hoping to get from the negotiations.

items.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote

France 24s Claire Paccalin has the latest.

Gomez was charged with embezzlement, influence peddling, corruption in business dealings and misappropriation of funds, the ruling added.

items.title: Fujimori leads chaotic Peru vote as election officials arrested over delays

Sunday's vote saw a series of logistical foul-ups in delivering election materials that caused polling centers to open hours late. Police raided the headquarters of the National Office of Electoral Processes and later detained an election official over the delays.

items.title: Residents of Iraqi Kurdistan in despair: 'We weren't at war with anyone'
items.description: After a month and a half spent living under the threat of drones, residents of Iraqi Kurdistan are exhausted. Just 24 hours before the ceasefire, a civilian was killed in a drone attack blamed on Iran by Kurdish authorities. In total, around 700 drone and missile attacks have targeted US positions and certain Kurdish interests. Victims' loved ones are devastated by the war between Iran and the United States, of which they are the indirect victims. FRANCE 24's Marie-Charlotte Roupie reports, with Josh Vardey.

items.title: Hungary for change: On Victor Orban’s ouster
items.description: Peter Magyar’s win may reverse hard-right domestic and foreign policies

items.title: Middle East War: Iran threatens Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman
items.description: Iran's military has warned that no ports in the Persian gulf or Gulf of Oman will be safe if its own ports are threatened. It comes as the US military announced it will launch a blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas.

items.title: Under pressure: Tracking the pain in G7 government debt
InterCement deal ‘once in a lifetime opportunity,’ Mindlin says
items.description: Argentine energy mogul Marcelo Mindlin is leading a group of investors and creditors seeking to restructure Brazil’s InterCement, the parent company of Loma Negra SA. Leer más

items.title: Prosecutors probe US$10,000 New York flights linked to Cabinet Chief Adorni

items.title: What impact will the Iran war have on the wedding industry?
items.description: The wedding industry in the UK is gauging the impact of the war with Iran. Despite riding fuel prices putting pressure on supply chains, vendors say they are prepared to absorb higher costs.

items.title: Benin awaits the name of its next president [Africanews Today]
items.description: In Benin, the focus is now on counting the votes following Sunday’s presidential election. As for the outcome of the race, analysts say there is little chance of a surprise result between Romuald Wadagni, the incumbent president’s handpicked successor, and opposition candidate Paul Hounkpé.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV begins historic Algeria visit on first leg of Africa tour
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has begun a historic visit to Algeria, marking the first-ever papal trip to the country and launching an 11-day African tour focused on interfaith dialogue and regional engagement.

items.title: Pressure on Adorni builds as investigators probe assets, travel, bank details
items.description: President Javier Milei’s Cabinet chief is facing mounting questions over his finances after journalistic investigations uncovered inconsistencies, unexplained asset growth and transactions involving third parties; Judge requests banking secrecy rules to be lifted to aid probe. Leer más

items.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
items.description: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

items.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
items.description: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: Italy suspends defence agreement with Israel

items.title: Inquiry told school to lose students if $1.5-billion waste incinerator built

items.title: News24 | Italy's Meloni issues rare criticism of Trump after his attack on pope
items.description: US President Donald Trump’s attack on Pope Leo was “unacceptable,” Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Monday, joining politicians of all colours in springing to the pontiff’s defence.

items.title: Authorities in Argentina say fatal school shooting linked online ‘TCC’ crime community

items.description: Peter Magyar’s landslide victory in Sunday’s Hungarian election has handed his centre-right Tisza party a sweeping mandate that will give it a free hand to enact reforms, bolster the rule of law and potentially unlock billions in European Union funding.

items.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages

items.title: World Bank sees Argentina’s economy growing 3.6% this year

items.description: US President Donald Trump told reporters that he is “not a big fan” of Pope Leo XIV, after the global leader of Catholics made a plea for peace.

items.title: World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends
items.description: At current trajectories, developing economies will ⁠generate only about 400 million jobs, leaving a deficit of 800 million jobs, for the 1.2 billion people who will reach working ​age in the next 10 to 15 years, World Bank chief Ajay Banga said

items.title: US farmers pay price of Strait of Hormuz closure
items.description: The Strait of Hormuz carries a significant share of the world’s energy and agricultural inputs. Disruptions since the outbreak of hostilities between the US and Iran are constraining flows of oil, gas and fertilizer, with ripple effects reaching far beyond the Middle East. As the US begins its naval blockade of Iran’s ports, America's farmers are already feeling the repercussions, a few weeks out of the start of planting season.

items.description: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

It came amid his escalating feud with Pope Leo, who has criticized the war that started with US-Israeli strikes on Iran as inhumane. France 24’s correspondent in Italy Seema Gupta takes a look at how some of the Italian newspapers have been reacting.

items.description: Spain has been one of Europe's loudest voices calling for expanding trade with China and treating it as a strategic ally, rather than an economic and geopolitical rival.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon talks: How motivated is Netanyahu to find a deal?
items.description: Lebanon and Israel are set to hold the first direct diplomatic talks in decades on Tuesday in Washington following more than a month of war between Israel and the Hezbollah militant. But while the talks may be historic, the Israeli government has been mostly “ambivalent” towards their outcome, says France 24’s correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky, although there are some signs that has started to shift in the last few days.

items.title: Wadagni claims landslide victory in Benin presidential vote
items.description: Romuald Wadagni has secured a decisive win in Benin’s presidential election, taking 94 percent of the vote according to provisional results released by the electoral commission.

items.title: Germany debates rape law to tackle AI and online abuse
items.description: Calls are growing for Germany to update its rape law after a celebrity case involving "deepfakes" caused an uproar. Campaigners are also pushing for a move to the "only yes means yes" principle of active consent.

items.title: New trial over Maradona's death begins in Argentina
items.description: A ⁠new trial over ​the death of Argentine football legend Diego Maradona will begin on Tuesday, with seven members ​of his medical team charged with negligent homicide nearly a year after a previous case collapsed in a mistrial.

items.title: Greek police using masked migrants to forcibly push other migrants back across border
items.description: Mercenaries recruited since at least 2020 for illegal migrant ‘pushbacks’ in Greece, BBC told

items.title: 'Very cautious optimism' as Israel-Lebanon talks set to begin
items.description: There is a sense of "very cautious optimism" in Lebanon, reports France 24's Cyril Payen in Beirut, as the country gets ready to hold its first direct diplomatic talks with Israel in decades on Tuesday in Washington, following more than a month of war between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group that has rocked the tiny Mediterranean country.

items.title: News24 | Carney crows as Liberals take full control of Canada parliament after by-election wins
items.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney’s party secured a majority in Canada’s parliament after two by-election wins, boosting his Liberals as they work to bolster a country shaken by threats from the US.

items.title: School shooting in Turkiye leaves 16 wounded
items.description: The assailant, a 19-year-old former student, later took his own life as ⁠police attempted to apprehend him

items.title: Wadagni claims landslide victory in Benin election amid calls for unity and reform
items.description: In Benin, presidential frontrunner Romuald Wadagni has swept to victory, securing 94 percent of the vote according to provisional results released by the electoral commission. The preliminary tally was published overnight, with final official results still pending.

items.title: Luxury brands face profits squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks Dubai Mall sales
items.description: Luxury brands in March reported sales drops of 30-50% at ​the Mall of the Emirates

items.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
items.description: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

items.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
items.description: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

items.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
items.description: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.

items.title: India, Oman discuss ways to boost trade, investments
items.description: India will reduce tariffs on Omani products such as dates, marbles and petrochemical items, under the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement

items.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
items.description: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: 'They are striking everywhere'
items.description: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.
items.source: Paramedics in Lebanon speak to BBC after Israeli strikes

items.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
items.description: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

items.title: Nigerian airstrike hits market in northeast Yobe state
items.description: At least 200 people are feared dead after Nigerian military jets struck a village market while ​pursuing Islamist ‌militants in the northeast of the country on Saturday night, a councillor for ⁠the area and residents said on Sunday.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: News24 | PHOTOS | Prince Harry and Meghan visit Australia in first trip since royal rift
items.description: Prince Harry and Meghan visit Australia for the first time since their royal split.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
items.description: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

items.title: Argentina tweaks FX rules after cost of getting dollars out surges
items.description: Milei government issues new rules for investors moving money out of the country after increased use of the parallel foreign-exchange market drove cost of trades up. Leer más

items.title: Sydney community radio station faces closure after uni funding dries up
items.description: 2SER, which was jointly owned by the University of Technology Sydney and Macquarie University, has given many aspiring broadcasters their start in the industry.

items.title: Hospital at centre of child HIV outbreak caught reusing syringes in undercover filming
items.description: Footage shows staff in Pakistan injecting without gloves and reusing syringes, but the hospital boss refuses to acknowledge it is genuine.

items.title: Cameroon's Catholics fear papal visit could pay political dividends for President Biya
items.description: As rumours emerged of a papal visit, some of Cameroon's Catholics voiced fears the trip would give longtime President Paul Biya a chance to polish his image six months on from deadly protests following his contested re-election.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates historic Mass at rain‑soaked Basilica in Algiers
items.description: Algeria hosted Pope Leo XIV on Monday 14 April for a Mass at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers, marking the first papal visit to the country.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Colombia approves plan to cull roaming hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
items.description: Colombian officials on Monday authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos roaming freely through a region in the center of the country, where they threaten villagers and displace native species years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

items.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
items.description: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

items.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against WSJ, Murdoch over reporting on ties to Epstein
items.description: The ruling marks yet another blow in the Trump administration’s efforts to manage fallout over its release of the Epstein files and the President’s attempts to use the legal system to chill reporting he finds critical of him

items.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
items.description: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

items.title: Is this Syrian doctor one of the 80% Germany wants to remove?
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has ignited a debate by declaring that 80% of Syrians would be repatriated. Does this mean that Germany also intends to deport those who are integrated and whom the country needs?

items.title: U.K. will not back blockade of Strait of Hormuz, says PM Starmer
items.description: Britain had minesweepers in the ​region, Starmer said, and ‌while he could not discuss operational matters, the military capability was “focused from our point of view on getting the Strait full open”

items.title: Environmental NGOs seek ‘largest collective lawsuit in history’ in defence of Argentina’s glaciers
items.description: Environmental groups call on members of the public to join a massive collective lawsuit against President Javier Milei’s changes to Argentina’s Glacier Law, which were approved by the lower house in the early hours of Thursday morning. Leer más

items.title: Benin leans into painful past to encourage cultural tourism
items.description: Benin is leaning into its painful history as a key port in the transatlantic slave trade to encourage tourism.

items.title: Trump uses McDonald’s Oval Office stunt to tout new US tip tax break
items.description: Donald Trump staged a McDonald’s DoorDash delivery to the Oval Office to promote new tax deductions on tips, touting relief of up to $25,000.

items.title: Cabinet chiefs past and present
items.description: The Cabinet chief’s role as whipping boy can be traced back to the post’s origins in the 1995 constitutional reform. Leer más

items.title: Pope in Algeria urges forgiveness and reconciliation on historic visit
items.description: Algeria welcomed Pope Leo XIV on Monday, 13 April 2026, marking the first visit by a pontiff to the Muslim-majority country, with a focus on interfaith dialogue and peace.

items.title: Founder of China property giant Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
items.description: Hui Ka Yan's plea comes after three years in detention, while Evergrande's liquidators are battling in court outside mainland China to freeze the offshore assets of the founder and his ex-spouse over billions of US dollars in dividends and remuneration.

items.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
items.description: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

items.title: Celebrities and fans pay tributes to Asha Bhosle
items.description: Asha Bhosle died in Mumbai on Sunday after being admitted to the hospital following a heart attack.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Sixteen injured in rare Turkey school shooting in Siverek district
items.description: Turkey was rocked by a school shooting on Tuesday 14 April 2026 when an 18 year old former student opened fire at Ahmet Koyuncu Vocational and Technical Anatolian High School in Siverek, in Şanlıurfa province.

items.title: 'It was me as a doctor': Trump 'repents' over AI Jesus post
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, April 14: There's outrage after Donald Trump's vitriolic critique of Pope Leo and a now deleted Jesus-like AI image of him. The Washington Post suggests that this latest controversy will alienate Trump's staunchly religious base. Also in the press: Amazon is accused of ignoring the death of a worker for several hours in a distribution centre in Oregon. Plus: an independent coffee shop shares its recipe for a drink and goes viral!

items.title: Ukraine, Russia renew drone strikes after Easter truce
items.description: An infrastructure facility was hit in the central-eastern Dnipropetrovsk region, Ukrainian authorities said

items.title: Pope Leo XIV pushes back as Donald Trump criticism escalates
items.description: US-born Pope Leo XIV pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s broadside against him over the U.S.-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration.

items.title: News24 | Orban concedes defeat to Magyar after 16-year rule in Hungary
items.description: Nationalist Viktor Orban, who has ruled Hungary for 16 years, on Sunday conceded defeat to conservative Peter Magyar in parliamentary elections.

items.title: News24 | Iran executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, as authorities ‘prolong their crumbling rule’
items.description: Iranian authorities executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, the highest number since 1989, two NGOs said on Monday, warning it risked using capital punishment even more extensively after protests in January and the war against Israel and the US.

items.title: IMF cuts growth outlook, warns of potential global recession if Iran war worsens
items.description: IMF's chief economist says that the Iran war has created a far bigger risk to the global economy than President Donald Trump's initial wave of steep tariffs did a year ago.

items.title: Lucrecia Martel: ‘Filmmakers have the best tools – cheaper and more potent than missiles’
items.description: Argentine film director Lucrecia Martel moves into documentary making with ‘Nuestra tierra’ (“Our land”), a feature which does not limit itself to reconstructing the facts. Leer más

items.title: Eduardo Coudet makes impressive start at River Plate
items.description: River are in the Copa Sudamericana for the first time since winning it in 2014 in Marcelo Gallardo’s first year in charge, a title which kickstarted one of the greatest coaching tenures in history. Now, his replacement would love to launch a dynasty of his own, with the cup as a launching pad. Leer más

items.title: Russian Memorial human rights NGO labeled 'extremist'
items.description: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

items.title: OpenAI CEO's California home hit by Molotov cocktail, man arrested
items.description: Police in San Francisco, a hub for tech development, said they had responded after reports that someone had tried to set fire to a gate at the sprawling home

items.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
items.description: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

items.title: Milei’s auction favours group with past China ties, US dredger warns
items.description: US dredging company calls out bid terms of President Javier Milei’s signature concession in Argentina, claiming they’re tilted in favour of a consortium with past ties to China. Leer más
